sections.name: Immediate Triage
steps: Check Search API health endpoint: curl https://api.finserve.com/search/health
Check CloudWatch dashboard: go to SearchAPI-Production dashboard
Identify which metric is breached: latency, error_rate, or availability
Check if issue is gradual (latency creep) or sudden (deployment related)

sections.name: Latency Investigation
steps: Run slow query log: SELECT * FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY mean_exec_time DESC LIMIT 20
Check for missing indexes: SELECT schemaname, tablename, attname FROM pg_stats WHERE n_distinct < 0 AND tablename IN ('documents', 'tags')
Check database connection count: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE datname='searchdb'
Check Redis cache hit rate in CloudWatch: SearchAPI/CacheHitRate metric
Check if ORM query count is elevated: look for N+1 patterns in APM
Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the top slow query identified in step 1

sections.name: Error Rate Investigation
steps: Check error logs: kubectl logs -l app=search-api --since=30m | grep ERROR
Check Redis connectivity: redis-cli -h redis.finserve.internal ping
Check if Redis connection pool is exhausted: redis-cli info clients | grep connected_clients
Check recent deployments: git log --oneline -10 on search-api repo
If connection leak suspected: check connection count trend over last 30 minutes

sections.name: Availability Investigation
steps: Check pod status: kubectl get pods -l app=search-api
Check pod logs for OOM kills: kubectl describe pod <pod-name> | grep OOMKilled
Check certificate expiry: openssl s_client -connect api.finserve.com:443 | openssl x509 -noout -dates
Check database connectivity from pod: kubectl exec -it <pod-name> -- nc -zv db.finserve.internal 5432

sections.name: Escalation
steps: If not resolved in 30 minutes: page on-call database team
If data integrity suspected: immediately disable search and page security team
If revenue impact > $10k estimated: notify VP Engineering
Create incident JIRA ticket with INC prefix and link to this runbook

sections.name: Immediate Triage
steps: Check payment health: curl https://api.finserve.com/payments/health
Check Stripe status page: https://status.stripe.com
Check failed transaction count in CloudWatch: Payments/FailedTransactions
Determine if failures are Stripe-side or internal

sections.name: Third Party Gateway Issues
steps: Check circuit breaker status: GET /internal/circuit-breaker/status
If circuit breaker OPEN: payments are queued, check queue depth
Check Stripe dashboard for their incident status
Monitor queue drain when Stripe recovers: queue should process automatically
If queue not draining after Stripe recovery: manually trigger queue processor

sections.name: Database Issues
steps: Check connection pool utilisation: GET /internal/metrics | grep db_pool
Check if batch jobs running: SELECT * FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE application_name LIKE 'batch%'
If batch job consuming connections: kill batch job with SELECT pg_terminate_backend(pid)
Check connection pool size vs active connections
Consider increasing pool size temporarily: update PAYMENT_DB_POOL_SIZE env var

sections.name: Escalation
steps: If failed transactions > 1000: immediately notify payments team lead
If customer data affected: page security team
All P1 payment incidents require post-mortem within 48 hours
Notify finance team of estimated transaction loss

sections.name: Immediate Triage
steps: Check auth health: curl https://api.finserve.com/auth/health
Check token validation error rate in CloudWatch: Auth/TokenValidationErrors
Check certificate expiry: openssl s_client -connect auth.finserve.com:443 | openssl x509 -noout -dates
Check memory usage: kubectl top pods -l app=auth-service

sections.name: Certificate Issues
steps: Check all certificates: kubectl get certificates -n production
Check cert-manager logs: kubectl logs -n cert-manager -l app=cert-manager
If certificate expired: kubectl delete certificate auth-tls && kubectl apply -f auth-tls-cert.yaml
Verify renewal: kubectl describe certificate auth-tls | grep Conditions

sections.name: Memory Issues
steps: Check cache size: GET /internal/cache/stats
If cache size > 40000 sessions: manually trigger eviction via POST /internal/cache/evict
Check memory trend over last 2 hours in CloudWatch
If OOM imminent: rolling restart kubectl rollout restart deployment/auth-service

sections.name: Escalation
steps: Auth P1s affect ALL services — escalate immediately to all service teams
Page security team for any token validation anomalies
If suspected breach: follow security incident playbook SIR-001
